proven.claim: Proven property: the value printed for “${issues.length}” is always equal to the actual length
proven.smt2:
; LINE: 518
; PRINCIPLE: P7  –  The log on line 518 must report the exact number of issues discovered.
; Proven property: the value printed for “${issues.length}” is always equal to the actual length
; of the `issues` array.  We prove the negation is UNSAT.

;--- Preconditions (state before the log)
(declare-const issues_len Int)          ; = issues.length
(declare-const dryRunFlag Int)          ; 0 = false, 1 = true
(assert (>= issues_len 0))              ; array length cannot be negative
(assert (or (= dryRunFlag 0) (= dryRunFlag 1))) ; boolean encoded as 0/1

;--- Transition (the code does not modify `issues_len` before the log)
; (no state change – the log simply reads `issues_len`)

;--- Log expression (what the log *claims* it prints)
(declare-const loggedCount Int)         ; the number rendered in the message
(assert (= loggedCount issues_len))      ; the implementation: console.log prints issues.length

;--- Negated property we try to refute:
; “the logged count differs from the real count”
(assert (not (= loggedCount issues_len)))

(check-sat)
; Expected result: unsat  → the property holds for all reachable states.
